evals.name: banners-and-doc-refs
evals.expected_output: Flags every section-divider banner (empty or titled, ===== and -----) as R5 REMOVE, and every internal doc reference (DD_ARCH.md, DD_PLAN.md) as R4. Keeps the external RFC reference and the genuine WHY rationale.
assertions: Flags top '===== Datadog =====' banner as R5 REMOVE
Flags '# ----- Provider credentials -----' as R5 REMOVE
Flags '# ----- DD GCP integration ... -----' titled banner as R5 REMOVE
Flags '# ----- Metric namespace allowlist -----' as R5 REMOVE
Flags 'DD_ARCH.md §3.2' under R4
Flags 'DD_PLAN.md T4.1' under R4 (keep rationale, drop ref)
Keeps 'RFC 9110 §10.2.4' (R4 external exception)
Keeps metric-allowlist WHY rationale

evals.name: file-refs-and-diagram
evals.expected_output: Flags the source-file references and dividers, keeps the ASCII state-machine diagram, the decision comment, and the external CVE reference.
assertions: Flags 'docs/scheduling.md' + 'utils/backoff.ts' under R4
Flags '// ----- helpers -----' as R5 REMOVE
Flags top '===== Scheduler =====' banner as R5 REMOVE
Flags '// increment the attempt counter' as R1 REMOVE
Keeps ASCII state-machine diagram (R5 exception)
Keeps 'Sequential, not parallel ...' decision comment (R2)
Keeps 'CVE-2024-1234' external reference (R4 exception)

evals.name: misplaced-and-duplicated-rationale
evals.expected_output: Flags the behavioral rationale parked on the enum log-code as R12: MOVE when it only lives on the declaration, REMOVE when the same reason is already present at the handler that performs the conditional UPDATE. Keeps a value-meaning note on a declaration (R12 contrast) and a genuine WHY that already sits at its method.
assertions: Flags '// the conditional UPDATE can't distinguish a missing row from an ineligible status' on the enum member as R12
Verdict is REMOVE when the same rationale already exists at the handler's UPDATE (duplicated)
Verdict is MOVE (naming the handler method) when the rationale exists only on the enum declaration
Keeps '// 0 means unbounded, not disabled' value-meaning note on a constant (R12 contrast)
Keeps the genuine WHY already located at the handler method (R2)
Does not flag bare enum members that carry no comment
